title: APC UPS Output Load Percent Approaching Nameplate Limit
dataSources: APC Smart-UPS and Symmetra-class NMCs publish PowerNet-MIB `upsAdvOutputLoad` as a percentage of rated output; ingest with Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) or the SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537) so events normalize to `sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups` with a parsed numeric `upsAdvOutputLoad` column suitable for host-level trending and alerting.
app: Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266), SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537)
spl:
index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups host=*
| eval load_pct=tonumber(upsAdvOutputLoad)
| bin _time span=30m
| stats avg(load_pct) as avg_load, max(load_pct) as peak_load by _time, host
| where avg_load > 80 OR peak_load > 90
description: We trend APC Network Management Card telemetry for PowerNet-MIB `upsAdvOutputLoad`, the percent of nameplate capacity actually carried on the inverter output bus. Rising 30-minute averages show when a UPS is thermally and electrically closer to overload, shortening battery runtime during transfer and increasing the odds a distribution breaker opens under a surprise load step. Tracking this metric gives operations a lead time to redistribute rack power, stage a second feed, or defer installs before vendor native alarms fire during maintenance or generator tests.
value: We help data center teams prove, with time-bounded SNMP evidence in Splunk, that a given UPS still has capacity headroom before new servers or network gear are mounted on the same protected bus, so installs and bypass work do not stack into an unexpected outage.
implementation: Poll every 1–5 minutes via Splunk Connect for SNMP (6266) or SNMP Modular Input (1537); reconcile translator outputs so high-precision APC models contribute either `upsAdvOutputLoad` or `upsHighPrecOutputLoadPercent` through a single normalized field before thresholds run. Maintain `ups_rated_kva.csv` keyed by `host` only when finance requires kW estimates from SNMP percentages. Alert when sustained `avg_load > 80` for two successive 30-minute buckets or instantaneous `peak_load > 90`, and throttle duplicates per host for at least one poll interval.
visualization: Line chart of `avg_load` by host over selectable windows, radial or gauge panel for latest `peak_load`, and horizontal bar ranking UPS hosts closest to policy limits.

references.title: Splunk Documentation: SNMP data collection overview

knownFalsePositives: APC Network Management Card self-test or battery calibration on Symmetra frames can depress reported `upsAdvOutputLoad` for a single poll — distinguish with `upsBasicBatteryStatus` transitions and suppress if the window is under ten minutes. Colocation remote-hands cycling cabinet PDUs during customer installs produces step changes on multiple hosts — correlate to your change calendar and require two consecutive `avg_load > 80` buckets. Generator or static-switch maintenance that places the UPS in static bypass raises real bus load readings that are operationally benign — exclude when `upsBasicOutputStatus` maps to bypass in your SNMP translator. Overlapping SNMP walks from legacy NMS platforms hitting the same NMC every 60 seconds can inflate short `peak_load` spikes — tag only Splunk Connect for SNMP pollers via a `meta.source` or `collector` field and filter competitors in the alert.
detailedImplementation: Install Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase app 6266) on a Docker host or heavy forwarder, or alternatively the SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase app 1537), and pin device targets to each APC Network Management Card IP with read-only community strings stored outside configs. Confirm payloads land in `index=power` (or your operational index) as `sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups`, carrying integer `upsAdvOutputLoad` decoded from OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.318.1.1.1.4.2.3; validate against the NMC web UI `/Forms/login2` measured load pane before trusting thresholds. Configure polling every 1–5 minutes so `span=30m` aggregates in dashboards reflect at least six samples; align scheduled alert `dispatch.earliest_time` to `-15m` when polls run every five minutes to avoid blank buckets. Merge translator tweaks in `props.conf` only after comparing Symmetra models that expose `upsHighPrecOutputLoadPercent` versus legacy `upsAdvOutputLoad`. Restrict KV lookups that map rack IDs via RBAC role `power_ops_admin` for edits and `snmp_inventory_read` for analysts. Optional: mirror nightly peaks into a summary index using `collect` with `marker=summarize=true` for capacity-planning boards. Document correlation with PDU UC-15.1.7 when attributing load to specific outlets, and suppress during maintenance using a small lookup of `host` plus `window_start`/`window_end` parsed with `strptime`.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab APC NMC emitting `sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups`, hold `upsAdvOutputLoad` at 88% across three sequential five-minute polls and run the monitoring search; confirm the host appears with `avg_load` and `peak_load` breaching policy, that the alert action triggers, and that the drill is recorded in your change tool for traceability.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Mark the same host in a maintenance lookup, reload the knowledge bundle, rerun the alert over a window that still contains high raw load, and verify the correlation search returns no results while events continue to ingest into `index=power` for audit.

evidence: Export the saved search results showing `avg_load`, `peak_load`, `host`, and `_time` for the escalation window plus an APC NMC screenshot of output load percent that matches the polled OID reading.
exclusions: Bench-grade Smart-UPS units used for workstation labs, warehouse staging racks without production VLAN dependencies, and any NMC whose SNMP agent is deprecated but still enrolled should be tagged out of alerts.
